note-sounded-pitch.text: Derivable from <pitch> combined with <transpose>; no single sounded-pitch element

note-pitch-alter.text: <alter> with integer semitone values (e.g., -1 for flat, 1 for sharp)

note-pitch-alter-microtone.text: <alter> with decimal values (e.g., 0.5 for quarter-tone sharp)

note-pitch-enharmonic.text: <pitch> encodes the exact spelling via <step> and <alter>

note-pitch-staff-position.text: default-y attribute on <note> can offset vertical position, but no dedicated staff-position override

note-ottava-cross-barline.text: <octave-shift> with type="up"/"down" to start, type="stop" to end; can span multiple measures

note-ottava-endpoint-hook.text: No explicit hook style attribute; dash-length and space-length control the dashed line appearance

note-unpitched-percussion-map.text: <score-instrument>, <midi-instrument>, and <midi-unpitched> map staff positions to MIDI notes

note-unpitched-notehead-map.text: <notehead> on each percussion note specifies the shape (x, diamond, triangle, etc.)

note-bend-target.text: <bend> with <bend-alter> specifying the target interval in semitones

note-bend-microtone-arrow.text: <bend> with fractional <bend-alter> values; no dedicated arrow glyph element

note-tuning-concert-freq.text: Not supported; no element for concert pitch reference frequency

note-tuning-scordatura.text: <scordatura> with <accord> elements specifying each string's tuning

note-tuning-scordatura-display.text: Not supported; no mechanism to switch between fingered and sounding pitch display

note-tuning-system.text: Not supported; no element for custom tuning system definitions

note-accidental-micro-stein-zimmermann.text: Quarter-tone accidentals (quarter-sharp, quarter-flat, etc.) are Tartini-style; Stein-Zimmermann glyphs available via smufl attribute with value "other"

note-accidental-micro-helmholtz-ellis.text: <accidental> with value "other" and smufl attribute referencing HEJI glyphs

note-accidental-micro-sagittal.text: <accidental> with value "other" and smufl attribute referencing Sagittal glyphs

note-accidental-micro-johnston.text: <accidental> with value "other" and smufl attribute referencing Johnston glyphs

note-accidental-micro-wyschnegradsky.text: <accidental> with value "other" and smufl attribute referencing Wyschnegradsky glyphs

note-accidental-micro-sims.text: <accidental> with value "other" and smufl attribute referencing Sims glyphs

note-accidental-micro-maneri.text: <accidental> with value "other" and smufl attribute referencing Maneri-Sims glyphs

note-accidental-micro-arbitrary-cents.text: <alter> encodes the cent deviation numerically; no dedicated visual cent-value display element

note-accidental-turkish-comma.text: <accidental> with values "slash-quarter-sharp", "slash-sharp", "slash-flat", "double-slash-flat", and numbered sharp/flat values

note-accidental-display.text: Presence or absence of <accidental> controls display; cautionary attribute marks courtesy accidentals

note-accidental-ficta.text: <accidental> editorial attribute combined with parentheses or bracket

note-accidental-smufl-glyph.text: <accidental> smufl attribute references a specific SMuFL glyph name

note-accidental-chord-stacking.text: Not supported; accidental stacking order is left to rendering applications

note-accidental-scope-measure.text: Not encoded; accidental scope rules are left to rendering applications

note-accidental-scope-octave.text: Not encoded; accidental scope rules are left to rendering applications

note-accidental-scope-all-octaves.text: Not encoded; accidental scope rules are left to rendering applications

note-accidental-scope-single-note.text: Not encoded; accidental scope rules are left to rendering applications

note-accidental-cancel-barline.text: Not encoded; cancellation rules are left to rendering applications

note-accidental-cancel-keysig.text: Not encoded; cancellation rules are left to rendering applications

note-accidental-persist-tied.text: Not encoded; tied accidental persistence is left to rendering applications

note-accidental-grace-affect-main.text: Not encoded; grace note accidental propagation is left to rendering applications

note-tie-start.text: <tie type="start"> for sound; <tied type="start"> for notation

note-tie-end.text: <tie type="stop"> for sound; <tied type="stop"> for notation

note-tie-chain.text: Each note in the chain has both <tie type="stop"> and <tie type="start">

note-tie-target-id.text: Not supported; ties are matched by pitch and voice, not by explicit ID reference

note-tie-incoming.text: Two <tied> elements on the same note: type="start" then type="stop"

note-tie-enharmonic.text: Supported; <tied> can join enharmonically equivalent notes with different <step> values

note-tie-across-barline.text: Supported; <tie>/<tied> start on last note of one measure and stop on first note of the next

note-tie-across-system.text: <tied type="continue"> for cross-system formatting with additional bezier data

note-tie-across-repeat.text: <tie> time-only attribute specifies which repeat passes the tie applies to

note-tie-across-volta.text: Two <tied> elements on same note (start then stop) represent ties into/out of endings

note-tie-across-jump.text: Two <tied> elements on same note (start then stop) can represent visual ties; no explicit jump-tie semantics

note-tie-into-coda.text: Two <tied> elements on same note (start then stop) can represent visual ties; no explicit coda-tie semantics

note-tie-chord-multiple.text: Each note in a chord can have its own <tie>/<tied> elements

note-tie-chord-partial.text: Only the chord notes that need ties receive <tie>/<tied> elements

note-tie-chord-seconds.text: Supported structurally; visual arrangement of overlapping ties is left to rendering applications

note-tie-chord-unison.text: Ties are matched by pitch and voice; unison ties in different voices may require careful voice assignment

note-tie-cross-staff.text: Supported via <staff> element on notes; tie curve placement is left to rendering applications

note-tie-cross-voice.text: Not supported; ties are matched within the same voice

note-tie-endpoint.text: <tied> default-x and default-y attributes position endpoints relative to the notehead

note-tie-curvature.text: <tied> bezier-x, bezier-y, bezier-x2, bezier-y2 attributes control curve shape

event-accent-staccato.text: Encoded as separate <accent> and <staccato> within <articulations>; no single combined element

event-marcato-staccato.text: Encoded as separate <strong-accent> and <staccato> within <articulations>; no single combined element

event-marcato-tenuto.text: Encoded as separate <strong-accent> and <tenuto> within <articulations>; no single combined element

event-tenuto-accent.text: Encoded as separate <tenuto> and <accent> within <articulations>; no single combined element

event-articulation-stacking.text: Document order within <articulations> implies stacking; no explicit ordering attribute

event-turn-accidentals.text: <accidental-mark> within <ornaments> for upper and lower notes of the turn

event-mordent-double.text: <mordent> or <inverted-mordent> with long attribute set to "yes"

event-nachschlag.text: No dedicated element; typically encoded as grace notes after the main note

event-tremolo-strokes.text: <tremolo> text content specifies number of strokes (0–8)

event-tremolo-multi.text: <tremolo> with type="start" on first note and type="stop" on second

event-buzz-roll.text: <tremolo> with type="unmeasured" and smufl attribute for buzz roll glyph

event-grace-beaming.text: <beam> elements on grace notes, independent from main note beaming

event-grace-chord.text: <grace> notes with <chord> element for grace note chords

event-grace-after.text: No explicit after-note grace; encoded by placing grace notes after the main note in document order

event-grace-slur.text: <slur> on grace notes, same as regular notes

event-hairpin-niente-start.text: <wedge> niente attribute on the starting wedge

event-hairpin-niente-end.text: <wedge> niente attribute on the stopping wedge

event-hairpin-to-dynamic.text: Wedge and dynamic can be positioned adjacently; no explicit link between them

event-hairpin-subito.text: No dedicated subito attribute; can use spread="0" on starting wedge for visual effect

event-hairpin-opening-width.text: <wedge> spread attribute specifies the opening width in tenths

event-dynamic-text-poco-a-poco.text: <words> element with "poco a poco" text within <direction>

event-dynamic-between-staves.text: Positioning via default-y can place dynamics between staves; no dedicated between-staves attribute

event-dynamic-alignment.text: <dynamics> halign attribute (left/center/right) and default-x for positioning

event-slur-cross-staff.text: Slur start and stop can be on notes assigned to different staves via <staff>; rendering is application-dependent

event-slur-cross-voice.text: Slur start and stop can be on notes in different voices; number attribute helps match them

event-slur-cross-system.text: <slur> with type="continue" for cross-system formatting with additional bezier data

event-slur-endpoint.text: <slur> default-x and default-y attributes for endpoint positioning

event-slur-shape.text: <slur> bezier-x, bezier-y, bezier-x2, bezier-y2 attributes for curve shape control

event-gliss-straight.text: <glissando> with line-type="solid" (default is wavy); or <slide> which defaults to solid

event-gliss-text.text: <glissando> or <slide> text content is printed alongside the line

event-gliss-cross-staff.text: Glissando start and stop can be on notes assigned to different staves; rendering is application-dependent

event-portamento.text: <slide> (continuous pitch transition, defaults to solid line)

event-arpeggio-cross-staff.text: <arpeggiate> number attribute links arpeggio across notes on different staves

event-string-lh-pizz.text: <other-technical> with text "+"; no dedicated left-hand pizzicato element

event-string-sul-pont.text: <words> with "sul pont." text; no dedicated element

event-string-sul-tasto.text: <words> with "sul tasto" text; no dedicated element

event-string-col-legno-battuto.text: <words> with "col legno battuto" text; no dedicated element

event-string-col-legno-tratto.text: <words> with "col legno tratto" text; no dedicated element

event-string-con-sordino.text: <words> with "con sord." text; no dedicated element

event-string-behind-bridge.text: <words> with appropriate text; no dedicated element

event-harmonic-pinch.text: <harmonic> can represent the symbol; "pinch" semantics encoded via <other-technical> or <words>

event-pedal-line.text: <pedal> line attribute and type="continue" for bracket notation

event-pedal-half.text: No dedicated half-pedal type; can approximate with <pedal type="change"> or <other-direction>

event-pedal-una-corda.text: <words> with "una corda" / "tre corde" text; <pedal> covers damper and sostenuto only

event-fingering-chord.text: Each note in a chord can have its own <fingering>

event-pluck-rh-fingering.text: <pluck> with text content (p, i, m, a)

event-pluck-lh-fingering.text: <fingering> on fretted instrument notes for fretting hand

event-harp-pedal-text.text: <words> with pedal text; <harp-pedals> produces the diagram, not text form

event-harp-bisbigliando.text: <other-direction> or <words> with "bisbigl." text; no dedicated element

event-harp-pres-de-la-table.text: <words> with "près de la table" text; no dedicated element

event-wind-flutter-tongue.text: Typically encoded as unmeasured <tremolo> or <words> with "flz." text; no dedicated element

event-wind-multiphonic.text: <other-technical> with appropriate text; no dedicated element

event-wind-overblow.text: <other-technical> with appropriate text; no dedicated element

event-wind-key-click.text: <other-technical> with appropriate text; no dedicated element

event-wind-air-sound.text: <other-technical> with appropriate text; no dedicated element

event-wind-tongue-ram.text: <other-technical> with appropriate text; no dedicated element

event-brass-mute-type.text: <words> with mute text; <sound> with mute attribute for playback; no visual mute-type element

event-perc-dead-stroke.text: <other-technical> or notehead "x" for dead notes; no dedicated element

event-perc-rim-shot.text: <other-technical> with appropriate text; no dedicated element

event-perc-cross-stick.text: Typically encoded via <notehead> with value "x"; no dedicated element

event-perc-choke.text: <other-technical> with appropriate text; no dedicated element

event-perc-roll.text: <tremolo> with type="single" and appropriate number of strokes

event-vocal-syllabic-stress.text: <words> or <other-direction> with stress marking text; no dedicated element

stem-direction-auto.text: Omitting <stem> leaves direction to the rendering application

stem-direction-voice.text: No automatic voice-based stem direction; <stem> must be set explicitly per note

stem-length-default.text: <stem> default-y attribute sets stem endpoint position

stem-length-override.text: <stem> default-y or relative-y attribute overrides the stem length

stem-thickness.text: Not supported; no stem thickness attribute

note-notehead-whole.text: Determined by <type> value "whole"; <notehead> can override shape

note-notehead-breve.text: Determined by <type> value "breve"; <notehead> can override shape

note-notehead-plus.text: <notehead> with value "cross" (plus shape); or "other" with smufl attribute

note-notehead-smufl.text: <notehead> with value "other" and smufl attribute for a specific SMuFL glyph

note-notehead-size.text: <note> print-object attribute and <note-size> in <appearance> for cue/large sizes

beam-secondary.text: <beam> with number 2–8 for 16th through 1024th note beams

beam-explicit-vs-auto.text: <beam> elements are explicit; omitting them leaves beaming to the rendering application

beam-group-by-beat.text: No beam-grouping rules in the format; beaming is encoded explicitly per note

beam-group-compound.text: No beam-grouping rules in the format; beaming is encoded explicitly per note

beam-group-irregular.text: No beam-grouping rules in the format; beaming is encoded explicitly per note

beam-group-override.text: All beaming is explicitly encoded via <beam> elements, giving full control

beam-subgroup-break.text: Secondary beams use separate begin/end values to create subgroup breaks

beam-over-barlines.text: Not directly supported; beams typically do not cross barlines in MusicXML encoding

beam-over-rests.text: <beam> with value "continue" on rest notes for beams spanning rests

beam-cross-staff.text: Notes with <beam> can use <staff> element to appear on a different staff

beam-cross-staff-inner.text: Individual notes within a beamed group can use <staff> to move to another staff

beam-cross-voice.text: Not supported; beaming groups are distinguished by voice

beam-cross-system.text: Not supported; beams cannot span system breaks

beam-slope.text: <stem> default-y attributes on beamed notes can indirectly influence beam slope; no direct beam-slope attribute

beam-thickness.text: Not supported; no beam thickness attribute

beam-secondary-gap.text: Not supported; no attribute for beam gap spacing

beam-grace.text: <beam> elements on grace notes, in their own beam group

beam-tuplet.text: <beam> elements on tuplet notes work the same as regular beaming

beam-tremolo.text: Double tremolos use <beam> between the two notes alongside <tremolo>

beam-fan-count.text: Number of beam lines at start/end of a fanned beam must be encoded via secondary <beam> elements; no single count attribute

rhythm-2048th.text: Not supported; <type> values range from 1024th to maxima

rhythm-4096th.text: Not supported; <type> values range from 1024th to maxima

rhythm-tuplet-mixed-values.text: Each note in a tuplet has its own <type>; <normal-type> specifies the base note value

rhythm-tuplet-with-rests.text: Rests within a tuplet carry <time-modification> like notes

rhythm-tuplet-with-dots.text: Dotted notes within a tuplet carry both <dot> and <time-modification>

rhythm-tuplet-with-ties.text: Tied notes within a tuplet carry both <tie>/<tied> and <time-modification>

rhythm-tuplet-nested.text: <tuplet> number attribute distinguishes nested tuplets; <time-modification> reflects cumulative ratio

rhythm-tuplet-cross-barline.text: Tuplet notes can span barlines; <tuplet type="start"> and <tuplet type="stop"> may be in different measures

rhythm-tuplet-cross-beam.text: Tuplet notes can have independent <beam> elements

rhythm-tuplet-cross-voice.text: Not supported; all notes in a tuplet must share the same <voice>

rhythm-tuplet-note-value.text: <tuplet> show-type="actual" or show-type="both"; <tuplet-type> specifies the graphical note type

rhythm-tuplet-beamed-hide.text: No automatic behavior; bracket attribute must be explicitly set to "no"

rhythm-proportional-spacing.text: No dedicated element; can approximate via <note> default-x positioning

rhythm-unmeasured.text: No dedicated element; can approximate using a single measure with no time signature and senza-misura in <time>

rest-2048th.text: Not supported; <type> values range from 1024th to maxima

rest-4096th.text: Not supported; <type> values range from 1024th to maxima

rest-multi-measure-count.text: <multiple-rest> element text specifies the number of measures

rest-multi-measure-width.text: Not supported; no attribute to control H-bar width

rest-tuplet.text: Rest <note> with <time-modification>, same as for pitched notes

rest-tuplet-full.text: All notes in a tuplet group can be rests with <time-modification>

rest-voice-offset.text: <display-step> and <display-octave> within <rest>; combined with default-y on <note>

clef-type-percussion-single.text: <sign>percussion</sign>; single-line display depends on <staff-lines> being set to 1, not the clef itself

clef-position-bar-start.text: <clef> within <attributes> at the start of a measure

clef-position-inner.text: <clef> within <attributes> after a <forward> or between notes

clef-position-courtesy.text: No dedicated mechanism; courtesy clefs depend on the rendering application

keysig-mixed.text: Non-traditional <key> with both sharp and flat <key-alter> values

keysig-microtonal.text: <key-accidental> with microtonal accidental values; <key-alter> supports fractional semitone values

keysig-modal.text: <mode> supports dorian, phrygian, lydian, mixolydian, aeolian, ionian, locrian

keysig-non-western.text: Non-traditional <key> can represent arbitrary accidental patterns; no dedicated maqam/scale system support

keysig-tonic.text: Not supported; tonic must be inferred from <fifths> and <mode>

keysig-change-at-barline.text: New <key> in <attributes> at the start of a measure

keysig-change-mid-measure.text: New <key> in <attributes> between notes within a measure

keysig-change-cancellation.text: <cancel> specifies the old key's fifths value for naturals before the new key

keysig-change-to-open.text: <fifths>0</fifths> with <cancel> for the old key; or <mode>none</mode>

keysig-change-courtesy.text: No dedicated mechanism; courtesy key signatures depend on the rendering application

keysig-change-courtesy-cancellation.text: No dedicated mechanism; depends on the rendering application

keysig-transposed.text: <key> reflects the written key; combined with <transpose> for sounding pitch

timesig-fractional.text: Not supported; <beats> is a string but fractional values are not standard

timesig-dual-stacked.text: Multiple <beats>/<beat-type> pairs; separator attribute controls display

timesig-polymeter.text: <time> number attribute applies the time signature to a specific staff only

timesig-polymeter-barline.text: Barline alignment is handled by the rendering application; MusicXML measures are shared across parts

timesig-polymeter-local.text: <time> number attribute scopes to a single staff

timesig-change-barline.text: New <time> in <attributes> at the start of a measure

timesig-change-mid-measure.text: New <time> in <attributes> between notes within a measure

timesig-change-courtesy.text: No dedicated mechanism; courtesy time signatures depend on the rendering application

timesig-change-cancellation.text: <senza-misura> to revert to no time signature

timesig-mensural-tempus-perfectum.text: Not supported; no mensural time signature symbols

timesig-mensural-tempus-imperfectum.text: <time symbol="common"> displays the C symbol but does not distinguish mensural meaning

timesig-mensural-cut.text: <time symbol="cut"> displays the cut-C symbol but does not distinguish mensural meaning

timesig-display-large.text: Not supported; no attribute for spanning multiple staves

timesig-metric-tempo-equation.text: <metronome> with beat-unit = beat-unit form (two beat-unit groups)

barline-span-partial.text: Nested <part-group> elements with <group-barline> control spanning subsets

barline-span-single-staff.text: <group-barline>no</group-barline>; barline appears on each staff independently

barline-span-grand-staff.text: Multi-staff parts have connected barlines by default

barline-display-thickness.text: Not supported; no attribute for barline thickness

barline-display-gap.text: Not supported; no attribute for barline component spacing

voice-count.text: Unlimited voices per staff via <voice>; each note/rest/forward carries a <voice> element

voice-id.text: <voice> content is a string; any value can serve as an identifier

voice-shared-notehead.text: Not supported; collision avoidance is left to the rendering application

voice-shared-notehead-diff-duration.text: Not supported; collision avoidance is left to the rendering application

voice-color.text: No per-voice color; individual notes can be colored via <note> color attribute

voice-hide.text: No per-voice hide; individual notes can be hidden via <note print-object="no">

struct-lines-arbitrary.text: <staff-lines> with any non-negative integer; <line-detail> controls individual line appearance

struct-repeat-forward-backward.text: Backward <repeat> on right barline of one measure, forward <repeat> on left barline of the next

struct-repeat-dot-count.text: Not supported; no attribute for number of repeat dots

struct-repeat-winged.text: <repeat> winged attribute (none, straight, curved, double-straight, double-curved)

struct-repeat-nested.text: Multiple forward/backward <repeat> pairs can be nested

struct-volta-text.text: <ending> element text overrides the display from the number attribute

struct-volta-cross-system.text: <ending> start and stop/discontinue can span across systems

struct-simile-count.text: Not supported; no attribute for displaying repeat count above simile marks

struct-simile-across-system.text: Multi-bar <measure-repeat> can span systems but display is renderer-dependent

struct-segno.text: <segno> for the visual sign; <sound> segno attribute for playback

struct-coda.text: <coda> for the visual sign; <sound> coda attribute for playback

struct-segno-multiple.text: <sound> segno/dalsegno attributes use token values to distinguish multiple segnos

struct-coda-multiple.text: <sound> coda/tocoda attributes use token values to distinguish multiple codas

struct-segno-placement.text: <segno> default-x/default-y attributes; can also appear as child of <barline>

struct-dc-al-fine.text: <sound dacapo="yes"> combined with <sound fine="yes"> at the end point

struct-dc-al-coda.text: <sound dacapo="yes"> combined with <sound tocoda="..."> and <sound coda="...">

struct-ds-al-fine.text: <sound dalsegno="..."> combined with <sound fine="yes"> at the end point

struct-ds-al-coda.text: <sound dalsegno="..."> combined with <sound tocoda="..."> and <sound coda="...">

struct-to-coda.text: <sound tocoda="..."> with <words>To Coda</words> and/or <coda> for display

struct-jump-with-repeat.text: <repeat after-jump="yes"> enables repeats after a D.C./D.S. jump; default is to skip repeats

struct-jump-without-repeat.text: Default behavior; repeats are skipped after D.C./D.S. unless after-jump="yes"

struct-custom-jump-text.text: <words> element for arbitrary jump text display

struct-rehearsal-enclosure.text: <rehearsal> enclosure attribute (square, circle, none, etc.); default is square

struct-rehearsal-auto-number.text: Not supported; rehearsal marks are explicitly specified, not auto-generated

struct-section-name.text: No dedicated section element; can use <rehearsal> or <words> for section name display

struct-section-boundary.text: Not supported; no semantic section start/end elements

struct-movement.text: <movement-number> and <movement-title> at the score level; multi-movement works use separate files

struct-movement-break.text: Not supported; movements are separate files

struct-measure-number-display.text: <measure-numbering> with values none, measure, or system

struct-measure-number-override.text: <measure> text attribute overrides the displayed number

struct-measure-number-offset.text: <measure> implicit="yes" suppresses the number (e.g., for pickup bars)

struct-measure-number-sub.text: <measure> number attribute is a string, so values like "12a" are allowed

struct-system-measures.text: No direct setting; achieved by placing <print new-system="yes"> at desired intervals

struct-page-orientation.text: No explicit attribute; orientation is implied by page-width vs. page-height

struct-page-number.text: <print> page-number attribute; <credit> with <credit-type>page number</credit-type> for display

struct-page-header-footer.text: <credit> elements with positioning for title, composer, copyright, page numbers on each page

struct-system-bracket.text: <group-symbol> (brace, bracket, line, square, none); <part-symbol> for multi-staff parts

struct-separation.text: Not supported; a MusicXML file represents a single fixed layout

struct-staff-hide-empty.text: No global setting; individual staves can be hidden per system via <print> with staff-specific layout

struct-staff-hide-empty-first.text: No dedicated setting; first system can include all staves with subsequent systems hiding empties via <print>

struct-staff-hide-specific.text: <part-name-display print-object="no"> combined with omitting content; renderer-dependent

struct-staff-ossia.text: Additional <clef> with additional="yes" and size="cue"; or a separate part with <staff-size>

struct-staff-ossia-scope.text: Ossia staff starts/stops are managed by part visibility per system; no dedicated scope element

struct-staff-cue.text: <cue> element on notes; <type size="cue">; can use a dedicated part with reduced <staff-size>

struct-tempo-poco-a-poco.text: <words>poco a poco rit.</words>; gradual text is free-form

struct-tempo-change-line.text: <dashes> for dashed continuation line after tempo text

struct-tempo-subito.text: <sound tempo="..."> at the point of change; <words> for display

struct-tempo-font.text: <words> and <metronome> font-family, font-size, font-style, font-weight attributes

struct-tempo-hidden.text: <sound tempo="..."> without a visible <direction>; or <metronome print-object="no">

struct-swing-ratio.text: <first> and <second> specify the ratio as integers

struct-swing-text.text: <swing-style> for style description; <words> for visible text

struct-text-system-attached.text: <direction> with directive attribute or placed on the top part

instrument-name-display.text: <part-name-display> and <part-abbreviation-display>; print-object attribute controls visibility per system; can also be overridden within <print>

instrument-change.text: <instrument-change> within <sound>; updates the virtual instrument for a given <score-instrument>

meta-title.text: <work-title> for the semantic title; <movement-title> for the movement name; <credit> with <credit-type> "title" for display appearance

meta-copyright.text: <rights>; type attribute distinguishes music, words, and arrangement

meta-dedication.text: No dedicated element; can use <credit> with a custom <credit-type> value or <miscellaneous-field>

meta-date.text: No dedicated element for composition date; can store in <miscellaneous-field> or a custom <relation>

meta-software.text: <software>; <encoder> for the person who did the encoding

meta-custom.text: <miscellaneous> with <miscellaneous-field> elements, each with a name attribute

lyric-word-part.text: <syllabic> with values single, begin, middle, end

lyric-multiple-verses.text: Multiple <lyric> elements on the same note with different number attributes

lyric-elision.text: <elision> between two <text> elements within a single <lyric>

lyric-on-rest.text: <lyric> can be attached to any <note>, including rests

lyric-on-chord.text: <lyric> on the first note of a chord applies to the whole chord

lyric-multi-syllable-per-note.text: Multiple <text> elements within a single <lyric>, separated by <elision>

lyric-hyphen.text: <syllabic> values begin/middle imply hyphens between syllables

lyric-hyphen-multiple.text: Hyphen display is left to rendering applications; no attribute for multiple hyphens

lyric-extender-endpoint.text: <extend> with type="start" and type="stop" to mark endpoint

lyric-extender-system-break.text: <extend> with type="continue" for cross-system extender lines

lyric-word-extension.text: <extend> after the final syllable of a word

lyric-verse-label.text: <lyric> number and name attributes identify the verse; displayed label is left to rendering applications

lyric-placement-between.text: Positioning via default-y can place lyrics between staves; no dedicated between-staves placement value

lyric-line-spacing.text: <lyric> default-y and relative-y attributes control vertical positioning per verse

lyric-alignment.text: <lyric> justify attribute (left, center, right); default is center

lyric-font-style.text: <lyric-font> in <defaults> sets default font per lyric name/number

lyric-syllable-formatting.text: <text> within <lyric> has font-family, font-size, font-style, font-weight, and color attributes

lyric-language.text: <lyric> xml:lang attribute; <lyric-language> in <defaults> sets defaults per name/number

lyric-rtl.text: <lyric> xml:lang attribute with an RTL language code; <text> dir attribute for directionality

lyric-spoken.text: <lyric> with <text> can encode spoken text; <laughing> element for laughing; no general spoken/stage-direction element

chord-bass-non-chord-tone.text: <bass> encodes any bass note regardless of whether it is in the chord

chord-polychord.text: Multiple harmony-chord groups within one <harmony>; arrangement attribute controls layout (vertical, diagonal, horizontal)

chord-repeat.text: No dedicated chord-repeat element; rhythm slashes encoded via <slash> in <measure-style>

chord-parenthesized.text: No parenthesized attribute on <harmony>; can use <kind> text attribute to include parentheses in display

chord-encode-text.text: <kind> text attribute can override display text; but <harmony> always requires structured <kind> content

chord-encode-intervals.text: <kind> value "other" with <degree> elements can spell out any chord interval by interval

chord-encode-duration.text: <offset> within <harmony> positions the chord relative to a note; rhythmic position is implicit from note ordering

chord-display-style.text: <kind> use-symbols attribute switches between letter and symbol styles

chord-display-minor-style.text: <kind> text attribute overrides display (e.g., "m", "min", "-"); use-symbols="yes" uses "-"

chord-display-maj7-style.text: <kind> text attribute overrides display (e.g., "maj7", "M7"); use-symbols="yes" uses triangle

chord-display-dim-style.text: <kind> text attribute overrides display; use-symbols="yes" uses °

chord-display-halfdim-style.text: <kind> text attribute overrides display; use-symbols="yes" uses ø

chord-display-aug-style.text: <kind> text attribute overrides display; use-symbols="yes" uses +

chord-display-superscript.text: Superscript layout is left to rendering applications; <degree-value> and <degree-alter> have positioning attributes

chord-display-font.text: <harmony> and <kind> font-family, font-size, font-style, font-weight attributes

chord-display-placement.text: <harmony> placement attribute (above/below) and default-y for vertical positioning

chord-display-alignment.text: <kind> halign attribute and <harmony> default-x for horizontal positioning

chord-display-region.text: No dedicated chord region element; rhythm slashes via <slash> in <measure-style> combined with <harmony>

chord-diagram-open-muted.text: Open strings have <fret>0; muted strings use <frame> unplayed attribute (e.g., "x")

chord-diagram-name.text: Chord name comes from the parent <harmony> element's <root> and <kind>

chord-diagram-custom.text: <frame> with arbitrary <frame-note> elements for any voicing

chord-diagram-inline.text: <frame> within <harmony> is positioned with the chord symbol

chord-nashville-diamond.text: No dedicated diamond notation; can use <notehead> with value "diamond" on rhythm slashes

chord-nashville-pushes.text: No dedicated push/anticipation element; can use <words> or <other-direction>

chord-roman-numeral.text: <numeral> with <numeral-root>; uppercase/lowercase controlled by <kind> and <numeral-root> text attribute

chord-roman-secondary.text: Multiple harmony-chord groups within one <harmony> (e.g., V of II as two stacked chords)

chord-roman-applied.text: <numeral-alter> for chromatic alterations of the scale degree

chord-roman-key-label.text: <numeral-key> specifies the local key for the analysis

chord-figbass-accidentals.text: <prefix> and <suffix> within <figure> for accidentals before/after the number

chord-figbass-slash.text: <suffix> with value "slash" or other style-value options

chord-figbass-stacking.text: Figures are ordered from top to bottom within <figured-bass>

tab-staff-6-string.text: <staff-lines> set to 6 with <staff-tuning> for each line

tab-staff-4-string.text: <staff-lines> set to 4 with <staff-tuning> for each line

tab-staff-5-string.text: <staff-lines> set to 5 with <staff-tuning> for each line

tab-staff-line-order.text: No control over string display order

tab-tuning-alternate.text: <staff-tuning> can specify any tuning per line

tab-tuning-display.text: Tuning is encoded via <staff-tuning> but display is left to rendering applications

tab-tuning-per-string.text: <staff-tuning> with line attribute for each string

tab-fret-x.text: No dedicated muted-string fret marking; can use <other-technical>

tab-fret-chord.text: Multiple <note> elements with <chord>, each with <fret> and <string>

tab-fret-ghost.text: No dedicated tablature ghost note; can use <other-technical>

tab-rhythm-no-rhythm.text: No explicit way to suppress rhythm display; <stem> can be set to "none"

tab-paired-staff.text: <staff-type> value "alternate" or multiple staves in a part

tab-slide-in.text: <slide> requires two pitched notes; no slide from indeterminate pitch

tab-slide-out.text: <slide> requires two pitched notes; no slide to indeterminate pitch

tab-vibrato.text: No dedicated tablature vibrato element; can use <other-technical> with smufl attribute

tab-wide-vibrato.text: No dedicated wide vibrato element; can use <other-technical> with smufl attribute

tab-trill.text: <trill-mark> in ornaments works on tablature staves

tab-rh-fingering.text: <pluck> with values p, i, m, a

tab-slap.text: No dedicated element; can use <other-technical> with smufl attribute

tab-pop.text: No dedicated element; can use <other-technical> with smufl attribute

tab-rasgueado.text: No dedicated element; can use <other-technical> with smufl attribute

tab-strum-arrow.text: No dedicated strum arrow element; can use <other-technical> with smufl attribute

tab-bend-release-rebend.text: Multiple <bend> elements: bend, release, then rebend

tab-unison-bend.text: No dedicated unison bend; can approximate with two notes where one has <bend>

tab-bend-arrow.text: Bend arrow display is left to rendering applications; shape attribute controls angled vs curved

tab-harmonic-pinch.text: No dedicated pinch harmonic type; can use <other-technical> with smufl attribute

tab-harmonic-tap.text: No dedicated tap harmonic type; can combine <harmonic> with <tap>

tab-palm-mute.text: No dedicated element; can use <other-technical> with smufl attribute

tab-let-ring.text: No dedicated element; can use <other-technical> with smufl attribute

tab-pick-scrape.text: No dedicated element; can use <other-technical> with smufl attribute

tab-muted-strum.text: No dedicated element; can use <other-technical> with smufl attribute

tab-display-fret-font.text: show-frets attribute on <staff-details> (numbers vs letters); <fret> has font attributes

tab-display-staff-line-spacing.text: <staff-size> controls overall size but not individual line spacing

tab-display-stem-position.text: <stem> has default-y attribute but no tab-specific stem position control
